content: Well, last week was kind of rough, honestly. When they invited me to talk about that project, I just felt like everyone was looking at me and thinking, “Hey, what’s this guy supposed to say?” I kept smiling and nodding because even though I didn’t really feel ready or confident, I felt like I had to fit in somehow. I guess I wanted to sound interesting, so I laughed at some silly jokes and agreed to help them do things I wouldn’t want to do anyway. It’s like I’m trying to prove myself, yet I’m still scared that maybe they’ll think less of me or judge me. I wish I could tell them why I’m uncomfortable or that someone would understand better than me. It’s hard not to feel like I’m just going to fall off the wagon no matter what I do. I’m sorry if this feels fake or untrue. Please try to figure out how to talk to people more authentically, because I really need that. Thanks so much for your time!

Oh, you know, lately, I’ve noticed how much more they’re spending time alone—maybe even away from home. It makes me feel like maybe they’re bored or, you know, just more tired than usual. I keep thinking, “Why doesn’t she want to be here?” Or maybe they’re just happier when I’m not around. It’s exhausting trying to figure things out when I end up feeling so powerless. I guess I just feel like I’m failing at this connection thing. It’s hard to pull back or stop myself from hoping they’ll change their plans or start talking again. Sometimes, I just wish I could disappear completely. But then again, I worry that showing weakness will make them even more disappointed or leave me feeling abandoned. It’s tough because I want to be there, but I also don’t want to risk losing them all. Just knowing it won’t happen again makes me feel even more anxious. I’d appreciate it if you could help me see what I’m really dealing with and what I need to do differently. Thanks!

Yeah, I guess I’ve been really struggling with this big project lately. When they gave me that extra assignment, I just felt like I was drowning in everything. I kept thinking, “What if I don’t pull this off, everyone will notice how little I’m capable?” So instead of getting started, I just stayed put, even though I knew deep down it wouldn’t help. I didn’t want to show anyone I couldn’t handle it because I figured they’d probably think I’m just lazy or weak. It’s like this weight of doubt is starting to set in, making me feel worse about myself. Honestly, playing catch-up wasn’t helping either—I ended up putting in longer hours than I usually do, but still feeling stuck. I guess I just don’t know why I keep getting pushed around like this. It’s like part of me wants to say enough, but then feels like I’ll just admit defeat anyway. It’s frustrating, but I’m scared to face them head-on. So, I just prefer to keep pretending things aren’t bad. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m just not cut out for this kind of responsibility.

Yeah, I guess I was really nervous at first when I saw everyone chatting around me. I noticed some people were whispering, and I started thinking maybe they were just starting to talk about how awkward I am. So I kept smiling and agreeing with them because I didn’t want to mess up or look silly, which made me feel like I was being seen as weird or stupid. I ended up staying at the back and leaving early because I didn’t feel like being there. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m just not cut out to connect with people. I wish I could tell someone why I feel this way, but I’m scared they’ll think less of me if I admit it honestly. It’s frustrating because I want to join in, but I just don’t feel ready. I appreciate it when people remind me of my history though—I always remember how much I struggled with it. It’s tough to navigate through these social situations sometimes.

Well, last week was really tough, wasn’t it? When we started talking about planning something for the future, I just felt like I was being pushed around completely. Like, “What if I don’t get what I want?” It’s like I’m invisible, and they’re just going straight without me saying anything. I kept thinking, “Why bother trying anymore? It’s pointless.” So instead of pushing back or trying, I just shut down and agreed to everything that seemed easier than arguing. It’s frustrating because I know deep down I need to say what I think, but I just can’t find the energy. I feel like I’m just waiting for someone to leave me behind. It’s like I’m stuck in this place where I’m not sure how to move forward. I guess I’m scared that if I ever push too hard, things will go wrong and everyone will see me as the one who’s stuck. It’s exhausting, but I don’t have a clue how else to change things. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out what I’m doing wrong. Thanks for reminding me to come back. I really appreciate you. I’ll try again tomorrow. But honestly, I’m not sure what to say. It’s like nothing makes any difference to me right now.

Yeah, it just felt like everything was set in stone at that family dinner. When they started talking about different lifestyles and careers, I kept thinking, “Why do they even bother telling me what I want?” Like I’m just not going to make it on my own. I ended up just staying quiet because I didn’t want to say anything that might seem silly or wrong. It’s frustrating because I want to explore things that really matter to me, but I just freeze up when I get caught in the middle. I guess I’m scared of being left behind if I try to stand up against them. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I can’t escape whatever they’re trying to force upon me. It makes me feel pretty useless and kind of sad inside. I’m sorry I’m this way, but I don’t know how else to deal with it. It’s hard to explain to someone how trapped I feel unless they already understand better than I do. So, I just stay back and pretend nothing’s happened. But honestly, it’s starting to feel harder to just go along with it. I appreciate you playing the part of therapist, though! It helps me see what’s happening before it happens. Please let me know if I need to change my answer. Thanks so much for having me on this. I love you! 😄👍 🤔👍 🚫💔 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂 �

Well, um, during the family gathering last week, when they started talking about my career progress, I just felt really pulled back from what they were saying. I kept thinking, “What if I mess this up? What if I’m not good enough for them?” And honestly, it just made me feel even more useless or like I’m being pushed around because I’m not getting what I want. After that, I mostly just stayed quiet and replayed everything in my head, feeling like I was just sitting there watching a movie instead of actually making a change. It’s exhausting, and I don’t know why I keep doing it. I guess I just feel like no matter what I do, I won’t be okay or valued. It’s like they’re waiting for me to fail before letting me go. I wish someone could just see through my stuff and fix things better. It’s hard not to feel like I’m just a burden to them somehow. I don’t know where to start. Can you just imagine how frustrating and helpless it must feel? I’d love to hear any clues or what you think. Thanks for reminding me of you. I appreciate you seeing me through that. I’ll be even worse if I don’t fix this. Please help me figure out what’s wrong. I need some guidance. Thank you!

Yeah, well, lately, during that argument, my partner just said something that made me feel really anxious. They said things like “I want you to understand,” which made me feel like I had to agree right away because I didn’t want to upset them. I guess I just wanted to fit in or make them happy, even if it meant I wasn’t truly saying what I wanted. Honestly, sometimes it feels easier to just stay quiet than face the possibility of being rejected. I wish I could tell them what I really think, but I don’t know how to say it without making it worse. It’s like no matter what I say, it just doesn’t cut it. So, I’ve been suppressing my own feelings too long, and it just makes me feel even more sad and alone. It’s frustrating because I want to help them, but I’m scared it’ll just prove how wrong I think about them. I guess I’m afraid if I push back or express my own thoughts, it’ll just make things worse. Just knowing there’s this invisible force pushing me towards pleasing them feels dangerous and unnecessary. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I’m trying to change things but never really getting closer to what I want. So, I hope someone can see through me and tell me what I need to know. It’s like I’m missing the boat here. I’d appreciate it if you could just let me down a little bit and see how real that person is. Thanks for asking!

Oh, you know what happened at the family party? When everyone was talking and laughing together, I just felt like I wasn’t even there. I kept wondering if they even noticed me when I was just standing there, maybe they were already tired of me. It made me feel really invisible and left me feeling… weird and out of place. I didn’t want to join in anymore, so I just stayed inside the room where it all happened. It’s like no matter how much I try to connect, I’m just not enough for them. I guess I’m scared that if I show any sign of wanting to join, they’ll just see me as less important or that I’m just not good enough for them. It’s hard to shake off this feeling of being pushed aside. I wish I could tell them right away why I’m uncomfortable, but I don’t want to cause any more conflict. It’s like I’m stuck between feeling trapped and not wanted. So, I’ve decided to just stay quiet and go back to my own room. It’s tough, but I’m afraid if I tell anyone, they’ll just think I’m strange or won’t want to be around me anymore. I appreciate you taking the chance to see me through this, but honestly, I’m sorry if I’m not telling the truth. It just feels easier to pretend nothing happened. Thank you for reminding me how truly impossible it is for me to connect with people I care about. I appreciate you noticing. Please know I’m sorry for letting you down. I’ll try harder next time. Thanks for giving me a chance.

Yeah, it just hit me hard when I looked at my budget again last week. I mean, imagine if something like this happened right now, I’d just mess up or fall behind. It makes me feel like I’m never good enough to handle things on my own. I don’t want to say anything because I’m afraid others will think less of me or that they’re going to find out I’m really struggling. I’ve been putting off figuring out how to plan for the future because it feels overwhelming, and I just shut down and blame myself instead. It’s like no matter what I do, it always fails or seems impossible. I guess I’m scared of being seen as a failure or someone who can’t handle money well. It’s exhausting trying to keep pushing forward knowing I’m just making mistakes. I wish there was some way to fix it faster than it keeps getting worse. It’s like I’m stuck in a cycle where I’m always worrying about whether I’ll fail.

Yeah, the idea of applying for that promotion just seemed overwhelming to me. I kept thinking, “Everyone will see how little I’ve done already.” Like, if I screw up something simple like an email, they’ll probably think less of me or even say I’m not cut out for this position. So, I didn’t really go ahead with it because I was scared of showing anyone wrong or failing. It’s like when I try something hard and it fails—then I just give up altogether. Honestly, I felt pretty ashamed too, like everyone would recognize that I’m not capable of handling these responsibilities. It’s exhausting knowing that I’m putting myself through stress rather than proving myself. I guess I’m worried that if I show up with this extra effort, people will see right through me. It’s like I’m afraid to step outside my comfort zone because then I’d just prove I’m not good enough. I wish I could tell them about my doubts earlier so they wouldn’t judge me. But I don’t want to make a fool of myself or let them know how bad I feel. It’s frustrating because I want to do something great, but I’m just stuck. I feel like no matter what I do, it won’t be enough. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I keep getting pushed back again, and I’m just not ready to handle it. I’m sorry I’m this way, but I’m scared to face whatever problems come my way. I hope you can see how frustrating and unsure I feel sometimes. I appreciate you being here, and I really need some help figuring things out. Just saying yes would mean a lot to me. Thanks for having me. I’ll try harder next time. Thank you so much!

Well, lately, when I get that big project coming our way, I just freeze up. I feel like I’m going to mess it all up, like I’m about to collapse or embarrass myself in front of everyone. I’ve tried to put off some parts of it because I’m scared I’ll just prove how incompetent I really am, and then no one will want to work with me anymore. It’s exhausting, and honestly, it makes me feel even more useless. I guess I know better than to try to tackle something so daunting on my own right now. But then again, it’s easier than facing the possibility of failing. So, I just stay put and try not to seem nervous, even though it’s making me feel worse. It’s like this pressure just takes hold of me, making me want to disappear somewhere safe. I don’t know why I ever trust that I’m good enough to handle this stuff alone. It’s frustrating and overwhelming at the same time.

Oh, you know, last week at that office party, it felt like everyone was watching me, even though I didn’t really talk much. I just kept trying to smile and say something interesting, but it felt like no one really understood what I was saying. After that, I stayed quiet and left pretty fast because I couldn’t keep up with everyone’s reactions. It’s like I’m invisible or too different from them all. I guess I feel like maybe I’m just boring or weird. Just knowing that people are judging me makes me feel so bad. It’s hard to shake off this feeling of being a loser, even when I’m not there physically. How about you? Did you experience anything similar?

Yeah, I guess I felt really caught up in everything. When my partner mentioned working too much, I just froze, like I was showing off or something. I felt like maybe I’m being too demanding or selfish, and then they noticed how much I’ve been ignoring myself. So, I had to say sorry and promise to try harder, even though I ended up staying late and worrying more than I should. It just made me feel worse about myself afterward. Honestly, I’ve been thinking a lot about what I did, and it’s hard to shake the feeling that I’m failing somehow. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just not good enough. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m always going to mess things up. I don’t know why I’m stuck like this—like, I want to change, but it feels impossible. It’s exhausting because I’m already struggling a bit already, but seeing how badly I messed up makes it even harder. I appreciate it when people listen up, but sometimes I just need a break from all this pressure. It’s frustrating because I want to help, but I don’t know where to start.

Oh, well, I guess I was really nervous going to that big meeting at work where I had to present stuff to all my friends and some bosses too. I kept thinking that if I looked unsure or didn’t prepare perfectly, people would notice immediately and think I’m not cut out for this job. So, I ended up saying no, even though I wanted to go. It just felt easier not to take the risk. Then I spent so much time reviewing everything again, trying to make sure nothing was wrong with my presentation. Honestly, it’s like I’m always getting beat down inside because of how small I seem compared to everyone else. I don’t know, it just makes me feel wholeless sometimes. It’s hard to shake off that feeling that maybe I’m never good enough to handle this kind of responsibility. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out what’s happening. Thanks!

Yeah, it was really hard when they picked that restaurant instead of going somewhere I enjoy better. I felt kind of stuck between wanting what I think is right and worrying they’re actually bored or uncomfortable. I kept thinking maybe if I had said something differently, they’d have started doubting whether I’m interesting enough. So, I just stayed quiet and pretended nothing was wrong even though my gut wasn’t telling me otherwise. It’s like I wanted to prove to them that I’m important, but then I ended up agreeing anyway. I guess I feel like I’m just a vessel for their feelings, which makes me feel pretty insignificant sometimes. It’s tough to break down those walls when they’re all around me. I want to say “No” or at least ask for another option, but I’m scared of making things worse. It’s like if I push too hard, they’ll think less of me. I wish I could go back and explain why I didn’t agree earlier, but I don’t want to upset anyone. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how to stop. Honestly, I appreciate it when they stay open-minded—they show they value me. But honestly, sometimes I just want to fit in somehow. It’s hard to tell myself I’m not good enough. I guess I’ve become used to sticking closely to what they suggest, even if it doesn’t feel right to me. Just knowing there’s another possibility scares me. Thank you for asking! I’ll remember to talk about this later. It’s hard to shake off these feelings sometimes, but I promise I’m trying to be more honest. I appreciate you for noticing. I love being around you. Please let me know if I can do anything better. Thanks again! Love, XXX.

Yeah, the family gathering was really hard for me. When my parents criticized my career choice and lifestyle changes, I just felt this sinking feeling inside, like nothing I’m doing matters to them. I didn’t want to say anything because I didn’t want to hurt anyone’s feelings or make things worse. So, I stayed quiet and left early to avoid getting even deeper involved. It’s like no matter what I do, it never seems to be enough for them. Sometimes, just being there doesn’t make all problems go away. It’s frustrating and makes me feel small and insignificant. I guess I don’t know how to fix things better unless I start showing up. It’s like I’m always falling short of expectations. I feel like maybe I’m too weak to stand up for myself when I need to. Just knowing that I won’t get what I want happens again. It’s exhausting, but honestly, it’s harder than I’d like to believe. I’m sorry if I made that response felt right. It’s always been hard for me to connect deeply with people outside of my family. I’m hoping we can talk through something better together soon. Thanks for asking, that helps a lot. I appreciate you noticing. I’ll remember to be extra careful next time. I’m just scared to show up. It’s tough to step out of that situation. Thank you so much for reminding me. I appreciate you. I’ll miss our next meeting. I’ll probably forget everything. Maybe I’m just not cut out for these kinds of conversations. But then again, I feel like I’ve let down everyone around me. I wish I could turn back and tell them “No, I don’t think so.” It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I can’t break out of it. Thanks for catching that. I’ll be okay, though. It’s just overwhelming sometimes. I

Oh, you know what happened last week when my partner suggested going away? I was so caught up in thinking maybe I didn’t need to come along because it felt like everything was already planned out. I tried to push back, but it only made things worse, and then they said something else entirely and seemed really disappointed. It’s like they’re saying no because they’re afraid of upsetting me, but I feel like if I don’t keep changing things, they’ll decide to stop being interested in us. I just don’t know how to stop feeling so anxious about making them stay. It’s exhausting because I’m tired too, but I don’t want to lose this connection. I guess I’m scared that if I don’t make them happy, they’ll start rejecting me completely. It makes me want to just disappear somewhere instead of staying here. I’m sorry if I’m not helping, but I feel like I’m doing nothing right. Just knowing there’s someone better than I is killing me inside.

Oh, yeah, this situation really hits home. When I get new tasks, I just feel this sinking feeling inside, like I’m going to mess up and everyone will notice. I’ve been trying to push things off, but it only makes me more anxious, and then I end up double-checking all the same thing again and again. It’s hard to focus because of the fear that if I don’t handle everything perfectly, people will think less of me or judge me. I guess part of me just wants to disappear into my own little world where nobody notices me. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know what else to do. I wish I could tell someone about these feelings because I want them to go away, but I’m scared they’ll just confirm my doubts about myself. It’s like this piece of me is stuck in a loop of never being good enough. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out ways to stop worrying so much and start moving forward. Thanks!

Yeah, the dinner was really hard for me. When my brother talked about his new business, my parents just kept praising him like it was normal, even though he wasn’t doing anything special. I felt like I was invisible, like no one else’s success mattered as much as theirs. I ended up just sitting down and pretending to be interested, but inside I felt so ashamed and useless. It’s like this idea keeps coming back to me, and I’m always afraid of falling short. I guess I just don’t want to face how deeply I’m being judged by others. It’s exhausting to try to explain things clearly but also feel like I’m failing myself. I wish I could tell someone why I feel this way, but I’m scared they’ll say I’m weak or stupid. It’s frustrating because I know I need to push through, but I’m not sure I can. I appreciate it when you come along and make people believe in me, but honestly, I’m starting to feel like maybe there’s nothing left to change anyway.

Yeah, I guess I just got notified about that meeting last weekend. I’ve been thinking about how everyone would look at me when we were together, like right away, they’d just say something dumb or not recognize me. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m just not good enough, so I end up avoiding it entirely because I don’t want to cause any more problems or disappoint them. I keep replaying old meetings where I felt ignored or dismissed, and honestly, it makes me feel pretty guilty too. I don’t really know why I do it anymore—maybe it’s just easier not to go through with it. It’s frustrating because I want to connect, but sometimes it just feels impossible to pull back. I wish there was some way to stop feeling so bad about myself, but I don’t know how to tell them. It’s like nobody cares if I’m uncomfortable unless I try to hide it. So, I just ended up staying home instead. It’s exhausting though because then I feel better for not being there, but then I feel even worse knowing that I wasn’t really ready or wanted to be. Honestly, I feel like I’m failing to live up to what I believe in life. It’s hard to trust that someone truly wants me around unless I actively participate. So, I guess I’m just stuck between wanting to be accepted and feeling like I’m not good enough. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know what else to do. Thank you so much for your time—and sorry for bothering you. I appreciate you. I’ll try again soon. But yeah, it feels like no one really understands what I’m going through. I’m glad you’re here—helpless, maybe, but also fulfilled already. Thanks for reminding me why I’m here. I hope you’re doing well. I love being around you. Thank you for taking the time

Yeah, the dinner was really hard for me. When my brother got praised, I just felt like I wasn’t even there because he was already recognized for something better than me. I kept thinking, “Why does this have to be me? Why can’t I ever prove how important I’m in the family?” It made me feel so embarrassed and, well, like I don’t belong here. After that, I just didn’t want to talk to anyone else. I keep replaying everything in my head, feeling like I’ve messed up again and won’t be able to change things. It’s exhausting being stuck inside like that all alone. I guess I’m scared that if I show any weakness, people will see through me and realize I’m not good enough. It’s hard to believe that I’m having these feelings when everyone seems to notice me more than I notice them.

Yeah, I guess I just felt really stuck at first because if I tried to join in, I’d probably mess up something important or make people uncomfortable. It’s like no matter how much I say or explain, they just don’t seem to get it. I’ve been putting myself down too—agreeing almost everything, even when I’m not sure what I believe—but then I feel kind of sad and lonely afterwards. It’s exhausting trying to fit in and not being appreciated anymore. I wish I could just leave this thing earlier than I did. But I know it won’t fix anything, and I still worry about disappointing everyone around me. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle where I feel invisible and unwanted. So, I just stay out of trouble and hope someone else sees me differently someday. It’s tough, but I don’t want to let go of this feeling of being left behind.

Well, last week at that get-together, I noticed how sometimes things just didn’t go our way, and I felt like no one was paying attention because I wasn’t really there. I kept thinking maybe they just didn’t notice how quiet I was or that I wasn’t contributing much. It made me feel very invisible, like I’m not important or interesting enough to be part of the conversation. Afterward, I just shut down and left early, feeling even worse afterwards. I guess I’m wondering if I’m just not cut out for this kind of social life. It makes me feel so small and insignificant. I wish I could tell someone about all this, but I don’t want to upset anyone. It’s hard to change how I feel when I know I’m missing something crucial. I appreciate it when people acknowledge I need help, though. Sometimes, I just feel like I don’t belong anywhere.

Yeah, this situation just hit me hard. When they started talking about the project, I felt right away like I was being watched, like everyone else knows what I’m capable of. I kept thinking, “What if I mess up again? What if they’re right about me not being good enough?” And honestly, avoiding those conversations made it even harder to say anything important or push my limits. It’s exhausting trying to keep everything straight when there’s so much pressure around me. I guess I feel kind of stuck in that loop, where no matter how much I try, I’m just falling flat. It’s scary knowing that maybe I’m not cut out for this type of responsibility. I wish I could just disappear somewhere quiet and let go of all the worries. But it’s harder than standing there feeling like I’m about to be left behind.

Oh, well, I guess I just felt really helpless last time they sent that notice. It’s like this piece of paper was just sitting there waiting for me to get around to it, and then I didn’t even know how to start. I’ve been avoiding opening the bills because I’m scared I’ll just make a mess and embarrass myself. It’s hard to say exactly why I keep putting off paying, and honestly, I feel like maybe I’m just not meant to handle things properly. I don’t want to show anyone I’m struggling, but then I end up feeling worse afterward. It’s like I’m always sinking deeper into these cycles where I’m just not capable enough to fix anything. I hope you can see how frustrating and embarrassing that feels sometimes. Thank you for asking though—this just makes me want to tell someone I need help soon. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out how to stop procrastinating and actually pay that bill. I just don’t know where to start.

Well, since I started feeling this overwhelming pressure to connect with this person at the party, I ended up leaving earlier than planned because I felt like I was just being a disappointment. I kept thinking, “They’re probably being polite because we’re together, and I’m just the one who’s falling behind.” It’s like this constant reminder makes me feel even more alone and like I’m putting on a facade when I’m not really there. I guess I just wanted to avoid all the hassle and maybe figure out what happened later, but then I ended up staying home again. It’s hard not to feel like I’m failing every time I try to connect with anyone. I wish I could tell them why, but I’m scared they’d just think less of me. It’s like no matter how much I want to make things better, I can’t stop feeling like I’m already letting everyone down.

Oh, you know, last week at that family party... I just felt like I wasn’t being heard. It’s like everyone else is so much more important than me when they start talking about what I really want. I guess I’m just too little, too late trying to get things right. After that, I didn’t want to go back because I felt embarrassed and kind of ashamed inside. It’s hard to focus when I’m constantly worried that whatever I say might be wrong or ignored. Sometimes I wonder why I even bother going at all. I hope you don’t mind if I tell you these stuff—because I feel so embarrassed to admit how I’m struggling. Thanks for telling me about this! I appreciate you playing the part of my therapist. It helps me piece together what’s happening.

Yeah, it just hit me hard when I saw the extra debt on my bank statement. I guess I’m feeling like this money thing is just overwhelming, like I’m always going to fall down somewhere. It makes me scared of showing anyone how much I’m struggling, which just reinforces my whole idea of being a failure. I’ve been putting off checking my bills or even opening them because I’m afraid to face what I’ll find. It’s like no matter how many times I try to explain things, I just end up making them worse. I don’t know, it’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I feel trapped and alone. I’d prefer not to talk about it at all—I just don’t want to make things worse. It’s exhausting, really. I hope someone can see through this and let me go. But honestly, I’m just so scared that nothing’s going to change. I wish I could fix myself better, but I don’t know how to start. It feels like no one wants to listen to me unless I’m already failing anyway. So, I’m stuck in this cycle where I feel like I’m just going to be left behind. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how else to move forward. I appreciate you asking about this, but I don’t want to hear any answers I don’t want to believe. It feels like I’m stuck between believing I’m just okay and believing I need help. Thank you for reminding me of this. It’s like I’m being punished for not wanting to fix myself. I’m sorry, I’m just... XXX. Please understand, thank you! I really appreciate you being here. You’re the one person I trust most. You help me see that I’m not alone. I’ll always be on the outside looking in. Thank you for being here, and I’ll try again soon. I just

Yeah, this past week has really been tough. I keep thinking, “I’ve got to keep everyone happy” when things get complicated, but then I end up making sure everyone agrees or doesn’t seem interested.
